title: Webserv
description:
Able to receive requests from several clients at the same time.
Must be configured using a configuration file, written in JSON and inspired by Nginx.
Able to process GET, POST and DELETE type requests as defined in RFC 7231.
We have taken as reference the RFCs from 7230 to 7235
Possible to upload and download files.
We decided to create a static HTML in which various tests could be carried out in order to facilitate the use of our server.
Logs are display in order to observe incoming requests as well as outgoing responses.

title: Inception
description:
Discovered Docker for the first time during this project.
3 Docker containers: MariaDB, Wordpress and Nginx.
Had to redirect the localhost to the name of the requested site, in this case tpauvret.42.fr.
I had an obligation not to use images from DockerHub (I could only import an Alpine or Debian image).
I used docker-compose to manage the different containers.
The Docker-compose file is written in YAML. It is in this one that I described the order of creation of the containers, their volumes, the network to which they have access, their environment etc.

title: Ft_containers
description:
Recreate the following containers: vector, map and stack.
Developed in CPP98.
I served source codes of the containers to understand how they have been implemented.
My containers are usable via the namespace ft.
I used different testers to see the effectiveness of my containers : - Tester 1 : - Tester 2 :
I also implemented my own tests. You can launch them using flag when you start my program.

title: Cub3d
header: Implement raycasting in a simple video game
description:
Game must be able to provide a smooth and sufficiently dynamic user experience.
Since multithreading is not allowed, raycasting had to be implemented intelligently.
The mandatory part requires you to be able to launch the game via a configuration file respecting a certain syntax.
Also, the player must be able to move with the WASD keys and rotate the camera.
We made the decision to add an exit to the maze.
In parallel with this project, I decided to create a maze generator in Python.
This generator allows you to create a map that can be used in our Cub3d.
The maze creation algorithm used is the Depth First Search Algorithm (DFS)

title: Minishell
header: Create a simple version of a shell
description:
Our reference for minishell development was bash.
We implemented some builtins ourselves, like cd, echo or even pwd.
Those that are not implemented are called directly via their path (like /bin for example).
Redirects, heredocs and pipe are also implemented by us.
It is possible to navigate, compile or launch programs, add environment variables as in a classic shell.
